# OpenCortex Memory

> MCP server for AI agent long-term memory with reinforcement learning, vector search, and multi-tenant isolation.

## Overview

OpenCortex Memory is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that gives AI agents persistent memory across sessions. It uses Qdrant vector search for semantic retrieval and reward-based feedback scoring to surface the most relevant memories over time. Memories are automatically deduplicated, decayed over time, and reinforced by usage.

Key capabilities:
- Store, search, and recall memories via MCP tools
- Reinforcement learning: reward/penalize memories to improve retrieval ranking
- Automatic semantic deduplication
- Multi-tenant project-based isolation
- Dual mode: local (self-hosted) or remote (JWT-authenticated)
- Graceful degradation: network/auth failures don't block user responses

## Install

Claude Code: `/plugin install` → select `opencortex-memory` → `npx opencortex-cli setup`
Codex CLI: `codex mcp add opencortex -- npx -y opencortex-memory && npx opencortex-cli setup`
Direct: `npx -y opencortex-memory`

Verify: `npx opencortex-cli health`

## MCP Tools — Lifecycle (mandatory every turn)

### recall
Search long-term memory for relevant context. Called before every response.
- `query` (required, string) — user message or search query
- `max_items` (integer, default=5) — max results to return
- `detail_level` (string, default="l1") — l0: abstract only, l1: + overview, l2: + full content
- `category` (string) — filter: profile | preferences | entities | events | cases | patterns | error_fixes | workflows | strategies | documents | plans
- `context_type` (string) — filter: memory | resource | skill
- `include_knowledge` (boolean, default=false) — search knowledge base
- Returns: `{memory[], knowledge[], instructions}`

### add_message
Record a conversation turn for future learning. Called after every response.
- `user_message` (required, string) — user's message
- `assistant_response` (required, string) — conversational conclusion only (no code/tool output)
- `tool_calls` (array) — [{name, summary}]
- `cited_uris` (array) — opencortex:// URIs referenced in the response

### end
End session and trigger knowledge extraction. Called on exit or when user says goodbye.

## MCP Tools — Memory Operations

### store
Persist explicit knowledge: preferences, facts, decisions, corrections. Auto semantic dedup.
- `abstract` (required, string) — one-sentence summary for ranking
- `content` (string, default="") — full details; 500+ chars triggers auto-structured overview
- `category` (string) — semantic type: profile | preferences | entities | events | cases | patterns | error_fixes | workflows | strategies | documents | plans
- `context_type` (string, default="memory") — storage type: memory | resource | skill
- `meta` (object) — arbitrary metadata (e.g., source, language)
- `dedup` (boolean, default=true) — enable semantic dedup
- Returns: `{uri, context_type, category, abstract, dedup_action?}`

### batch_store
Bulk import multiple documents.
- `documents` (required, array) — array of store-format objects
- Returns: `{stored, skipped, errors}`

### search
Search stored memories by natural language query with intent-aware retrieval.
- `query` (required, string) — natural language query
- `limit` (integer, default=5) — max results
- `context_type` (string) — restrict to: memory | resource | skill
- `category` (string) — restrict by category
- Returns: `{results[], total}`

### feedback
Reinforce or penalize a memory via reward signal to adjust future retrieval ranking.
- `uri` (required, string) — opencortex:// URI of the memory
- `reward` (required, number) — reward signal, range: -1.0 to 1.0
- Returns: `{status, uri, new_score}`

### forget
Delete a memory permanently by URI or semantic query.
- `uri` (string) — specific memory URI to delete
- `query` (string) — semantic query to find and delete matching memory
- Returns: `{status, forgotten, uri}`

### decay
Apply time-decay to all memories. Frequently accessed memories resist decay. Maintenance function.
- Returns: `{status, decayed_count, removed_count}`

### system_status
Health check and diagnostics.
- `type` (string, default="health") — health | stats | doctor
- Returns: memory count, storage stats, component status

## Memory Categories

| Category | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| profile | User profile information |
| preferences | User preferences and settings |
| entities | People, projects, organizations |
| events | Meetings, deadlines, milestones |
| cases | Use cases, scenarios, examples |
| patterns | Code patterns, design patterns |
| error_fixes | Bug fixes, error resolutions |
| workflows | Processes, procedures |
| strategies | Plans, approaches, methodologies |
| documents | Reference documents |
| plans | Project plans, roadmaps |

## Context Types

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| memory | General memories (default) |
| resource | Reference resources |
| skill | Learned skills and procedures |

## Configuration

Config file location (discovery order):
1. `./mcp.json` (project-local)
2. `~/.opencortex/mcp.json` (global)

```json
{
  "mode": "remote",
  "token": "<jwt-token>",
  "local": { "http_port": 8921, "ui_port": 5920 },
  "remote": { "http_url": "http://127.0.0.1:8921" }
}
```

Environment variable overrides:
- `OPENCORTEX_MODE` — local | remote
- `OPENCORTEX_TOKEN` — JWT authentication token
- `OPENCORTEX_HTTP_PORT` — local HTTP port (default 8921)
- `OPENCORTEX_HTTP_URL` — remote server URL
- `OPENCORTEX_UI_PORT` — console UI port (default 5920)

## CLI Commands

Setup & Health:
- `opencortex-cli setup` — Interactive setup wizard
- `opencortex-cli health` — Quick liveness check
- `opencortex-cli status` — Full system status

Memory Operations:
- `opencortex-cli recall <query>` — Search memories (-k for top-k)
- `opencortex-cli store <text>` — Store a memory (-c for category)
- `opencortex-cli stats` — Memory statistics
- `opencortex-cli feedback <uri> <reward>` — Apply RL feedback
- `opencortex-cli decay` — Apply maintenance decay

## Included Binaries

- `opencortex-mcp` — MCP stdio server
- `opencortex-cli` — CLI (setup, health, store, recall, status, feedback, decay)

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/StardustVision/OpenCortex-Memory
